mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.5 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer and housing demand, which drives upward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that outweighs rate-driven valuation effects, causing corporate margin compression and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or regime-conditional IC is not significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index exceeding 196.0 reflects accelerating inflation that forces central bank hawkish repricing, compressing equity multiples and generating negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances, or threshold crossing shows no statistical edge over baseline market drift.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding employment and wage income, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day returns with Rank IC < -0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive return frequency drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation without consumer absorption, leading to near-term earnings downgrades and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction model fails to outperform single-series baselines in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below its 6-month moving average signals acute production slowdown that triggers immediate risk aversion among institutional investors, causing short-term equity declines.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when INDPRO falls below its 6-month average, or signal loses predictive power after controlling for market beta.
